page: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-2 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the t2a server family. It is powered by an arm64 architecture ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor operating at a clock speed of 3.0 GHz. The instance features 2 dedicated physical cores (2 vCPUs) with a single thread per core, providing 8.0 GB of system memory, which equates to 4.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, and does not come with complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates that the t2a-standard-2 delivers average single-core performance, as evidenced by a stress-ng single-core score of 1812.29 and a single-threaded gzip compression rate of 24.01 GB/sec. However, multi-core and memory subsystem performance are generally limited. The stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 3594.57 and Geekbench multi-core score of 1997.0 fall into the weak and poor categories respectively. Memory bandwidth tests show 139.93 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 26.99 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Cryptographic performance is supported by hardware AES acceleration, though Passmark CPU encryption and physics tests register in the lower percentiles.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the t2a-standard-2 offers a low-cost entry point into ARM-based cloud computing, providing dedicated physical cores rather than shared-core resources. This makes it a suitable option for lightweight, scale-out workloads that benefit from ARM architecture efficiency. Recommended workloads include basic web serving, lightweight database operations, text processing, and simple containerized applications that do not require high memory bandwidth or intensive multi-threaded compute capabilities.
description: The Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-2 is an arm64-based general-purpose instance powered by a 3.0 GHz ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs (2 physical cores) and 8.0 GB of system memory, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show average single-core capabilities, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1812.29 and a single-threaded gzip compression speed of 24.01 GB/sec. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are relatively weak, as shown by a Geekbench multi-core score of 1997.0 and uncached read memory bandwidth of 26.99 GB/sec. This instance provides a cost-efficient entry point for ARM-native development, lightweight web serving, basic database operations, and simple text processing tasks that do not demand high-density multi-threaded compute or extensive memory throughput.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-2 is an arm64 instance with 2 dedicated Neoverse-N1 vCPUs and 8 GB RAM, offering entry-level ARM performance for lightweight web serving and basic database workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-2 is an arm64 virtual machine featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and a 3.0 GHz ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor.
tagline: An entry-level, dedicated ARM-based virtual machine designed for lightweight general-purpose workloads, basic web serving, and cost-efficient application scaling.
bullet_points: Powered by a 3.0 GHz ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor with arm64 architecture
Features 2 dedicated vCPUs (2 physical cores) and 8.0 GB of system memory
Delivers average single-core performance but limited multi-core and memory bandwidth
Lacks local storage and GPU acceleration
Best suited for lightweight web serving, basic database operations, and ARM-native development
